summary: Full regression for new split lead form + consent + cookie notice + Evohome legal footer. Backend: 29/29 pytest cases pass (incl. new consent/first_name/last_name 422 cases, click tracking for whatsapp/phone/email, end-to-end persistence in admin list). Frontend: contact tabs (general/visit), unit-detail enquiry, FAB brochure dialog, cookie notice (Accept All + Manage Preferences + persistence in gv_cookie_consent), footer privacy/legal/credit/disclaimer, admin CSV export columns all verified. No 'agent' wording remains on Contact / FAQ / Mortgage / Residences / Unit Detail. Fixed one critical backend bug introduced by the schema migration (downloads_service still referenced old lead.name).

minor.issue: Accepts the full LeadCreate payload — non-click lead_types could be POSTed here and bypass the form validations because /track doesn't restrict lead_type. Defensive hardening recommended (reject anything not in CLICK_LEAD_TYPES).
Returns 200 (not 201) on resource creation; functional but not REST-strict. Matches existing pattern across the API.

issues: Radix DialogContent missing DialogDescription / aria-describedby — a11y console warning persists from previous iteration.

action_items: (Optional) Reject non-click lead_types on POST /api/track to prevent the public endpoint from being used to create form leads without consent.
(Optional) Remove the now-unused 'agentLabel' string from /app/frontend/src/lib/constants.js — dead reference after agent-language removal.
(Optional) Silence Radix Dialog a11y warning by adding <DialogDescription/> to brochure and gallery dialogs.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED: /app/backend/services/downloads_service.py was still referencing lead.name on the gated-brochure path (line 43), which caused POST /api/downloads/{id}/access to 500 with the new schema. Updated to check first_name + last_name + email + consent, mirroring leads_service.create_lead validation.
constants.js still defines 'agentLabel: Grosvenor Vistas' even though no component references it any more — leftover from the agent-language cleanup.
routes/leads.py POST /track and POST /leads share the exact same handler signature/model. /track should explicitly restrict lead_type ∈ CLICK_LEAD_TYPES to keep the anonymous surface tight.
seed_data_creation: Several TEST_/TESTPersist prefixed leads created via pytest and Playwright (visible in admin leads list as TEST User / TESTPersist User / TEST LeadPatch / TEST Brochure). Anonymous Email/Phone/WhatsApp click events also stored.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Lead schema is now {first_name,last_name,email,phone,message,consent,project,lead_type,source_*,utm_*}. Backend enforces first_name+last_name+email+consent for non-click lead_types; /api/track is for click events only. /api/downloads/{id}/access now requires the same. Cookie notice persists via localStorage key 'gv_cookie_consent'. Admin CSV export endpoint is client-side built (frontend admin LeadsPage Export button) — header is: created_at,first_name,last_name,email,phone,lead_type,status,project,source_unit,source_building,source_page,consent,message. Use Playwright page.expect_download() to capture it. FAB brochure dialog testid='fab-brochure-dialog' with prefix 'fab-brochure-' on LeadForm fields.
rca of the issue: POST /api/downloads/{id}/access with a valid lead returned 500 because downloads_service.access_download still validated lead.name after the schema migrated name -> first_name+last_name. Updated to validate first_name + last_name + email + consent. Verified with TestDownloads.test_brochure_gated_with_lead_succeeds (passes).
